narrative:
The Zurich Zebras enter today’s slate with a potent core anchored by Pete Crow-Armstrong and Yordan Alvarez, who project as our primary catalysts with scores of 113.67 and 111.56, respectively. PCA is in a prime position to capitalize on a high SP skill matchup and a favorable platoon advantage, while Alvarez faces a soft target in Jack Kochanowicz, making him an elite play for power and OBP efficiency. Trea Turner and Junior Caminero also provide high-floor stability, with Turner’s elite BvP history and favorable platoon split against MacKenzie Gore positioning him to contribute across all five categories.

We are exercising caution with Kyle Schwarber and Christian Yelich, as both face left-handed pitchers today, triggering significant platoon penalties that dampen their overall efficiency. Additionally, Carson Benge’s deployment at the bottom of the order and a negative park factor make him a borderline play that warrants monitoring for any late-breaking lineup changes. While our heavy hitters provide a strong buffer, keeping a close eye on the bench for late-game defensive substitutions—especially for our catching tandem—will be critical to preserving our Roto standing throughout the afternoon.
